channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft Ends Support for Surface Laptop Go: What it Means for Customers
item.description: Microsoft has discontinued software support for the Surface Laptop Go, leaving customers without official firmware or driver updates. Learn what this means for users and potential alternatives.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Murder trial of tech consultant in death of Cash App founder Bob Lee begins
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The murder trial of a tech consultant in the stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee begins Monday, a year and a half after the widely admired entrepreneur was found staggering on a deserted downtown San Francisco street seeking help.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk's Starlink Internet free for hurricane victims through 2024
item.description: Following criticism, the company issued a new offer that extends the free service through the end of the year for victims of both hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: TikTok was aware of risks kids and teens face on its platform, legal document alleges
item.description: TikTok has been aware that its design features are detrimental to its young users and that publicly touted tools aimed at limiting kids’ time on the site were largely ineffective.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Will your AI bot put citizens at risk?
item.description: Government agencies have an obligation to provide accurate information to citizens, and a bad bot can have both legal and moral implications. The post Will your AI bot put citizens at risk? first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Key supplier of this critical semiconductor material is back in operation post-Helene
item.description: One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: DoT driving IT modernization to meet enterprise, local needs
item.description: Cordell Schachter, the Transportation Department CIO, said the agency’s DevSecOps capabilities are maturing as part of its digital transformation effort. The post DoT driving IT modernization to meet enterprise, local needs first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk unveils long-awaited robotaxi
item.description: Tesla CEO Elon Musk unveiled the electric vehicle (EV) company’s long-awaited robotaxi Thursday night, showing off a fleet of sleek self-driving cars with no steering wheels or pedals.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Lesser-known xargs command is a versatile time saver
item.description: The xargs command may be one that many Linux users don’t know, but it’s also one that can save a lot of time and trouble on the command line. It’s an unusual command with some very handy uses.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Senators press Commerce Department to boost transparency about CHIPS Act contracts
item.description: A group of senators are urging the Commerce Department to be more transparent regarding the terms of agreements made under the CHIPS and Science Act, arguing the agency must ensure the U.S. semiconductor industry is “safe and sustainable.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Biometric data: Is it safe to hand it over to any company that asks?
item.description: Biometric data, like face and fingerprint scans, can help you save time when accessing your personal data – but with it comes a whole host of privacy risks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Why did the 2024 Nobel for physics go to AI-related research?
item.description: The award this week of Nobel prizes in chemistry and physics to a small number of artificial intelligence pioneers affiliated with Google has stirred debate over the company’s research dominance and how breakthroughs in computer science ought to be recognised.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: TypeScript 5.7 improves error reporting
item.description: TypeScript 5.7, the latest planned release of Microsoft’s JavaScript variant with syntax for types, is now available as a beta release. With the update, the language’s type system is now able to report errors when variables have never been initialized.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: North Carolina maker of high-purity quartz back operating post-Helene
item.description: SPRUCE PINE, N.C. (AP) — One of the two companies that manufacture high-purity quartz used for making semiconductors and other high-tech products from mines in a western North Carolina community severely damaged by Hurricane Helene is operating again.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Cisco revamps key DevNet sandboxes
item.description: Cisco has expanded its DevNet sandbox environments to make it easier for network professionals to test and develop applications for key enterprise networking technologies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Astera Labs debuts Scorpio fabric switch to accelerate PCIe for AI, cloud
item.description: There’s a new type of switch that could soon be showing up in AI-optimized data centers, a PCIe 6 fabric switch. Astera Labs this week unveiled its new Scorpio fabric switch, which it says is the industry’s first PCIe 6 switch.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to use Java generics to avoid ClassCastExceptions
item.description: Generics are used in Java to allow types or methods to operate on objects of various types while providing compile-time type safety. Generics address the problem of ClassCastExceptions being thrown at runtime as a result of code that is not type safe.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft previews AI ‘building blocks’ for .NET
item.description: Microsoft is previewing packages geared to integrating generative AI services into .NET applications. Called Microsoft.Extensions.AI.Abstractions and Microsoft.Extensions.AI libraries, the packages are available in preview as of October 8.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Login.gov facial recognition option gets GSA approval
item.description: Login.gov will allow its users to match a selfie with the photo on a government ID, such as a driver’s license. The post Login.gov facial recognition option gets GSA approval first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Gemini Code Assist Enterprise woos enterprise developers
item.description: Google Cloud has announced Gemini Code Assist Enterprise, billed as an enterprise-grade tool that lets developers generate or transform code that is more accurate and relevant to their applications.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: American Water cyberattack renews focus on protecting critical infrastructure
item.description: CAMDEN, N.J. (AP) — A cyberattack continues to affect the largest regulated water and wastewater utility company in the United States, renewing a focus on the importance of protecting critical infrastructure sites.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SHIPKOWSKI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US considers asking court to break up Google as it weighs remedies in the antitrust case
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is considering asking a federal judge to break up Google after its ubiquitous search engine was declared an illegal monopoly, but it is just one of many possible remedies under review, according to a court filing.
item.dc:creator: SHAWN CHEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform harms kids' mental health
item.description: The social media platform is accused of deploying "manipulative features" to keep young users hooked.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Wi-Fi 7 brings enterprise-caliber predictability to wireless networks, says Cisco CTO
item.description: The ability to more effectively control wireless networks will go a long way toward making Wi-Fi 7 a viable upgrade for current over-the-air networks and, in some cases, even hard-wired environments. The evolution of Wi-Fi 7 has included more than a decade of spectrum changes and standards updates.
item.dc:creator: Network World
